title: Ambiguous `requires-python = "~=3.8"` in pyproject.toml triggers a uv warning on every tox run
summary: pyproject.toml declares `requires-python = "~=3.8"`. The tilde specifier without a patch version is ambiguous and uv prints a warning on every invocation (lint, static, unit) advising it will be interpreted as `>=3.8, <4`. The charm supports ubuntu@20.04/22.04/24.04 (charmcraft.yaml:32-34), i.e. Python 3.8 through 3.12, so the intended constraint is `>=3.8,<4` and should be written explicitly to silence the warning.
why_good_first_issue: One-line change in a single declarative file; no runtime/behavioural risk; clear done condition (warning disappears from tox output); reinforced by an in-repo pin comment that the project deliberately still supports Python 3.8 (pyproject.toml:24), so intent is unambiguous.
repro_steps: On VM cos: git clone --depth 80 into ~/work; export PATH=$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH; run `tox -e lint` and `tox -e static`; observe the first warning line emitted by uv. Also `sed -n '1,30p' pyproject.toml` to read the declaration.
repro_result: Reproduced on HEAD 8bfce19. Both `tox -e lint` and `tox -e static` (each OK overall) emit on their FIRST line: "warning: The `requires-python` specifier (`~=3.8`) in `cos-proxy` uses the tilde specifier (`~=`) without a patch version. This will be interpreted as `>=3.8, <4`. Did you mean `~=3.8.0`...". Confirmed source at pyproject.toml:6 `requires-python = "~=3.8"`. Expected: no such warning.
fix_hint: Change pyproject.toml:6 from `requires-python = "~=3.8"` to `requires-python = ">=3.8,<4"` (matches the documented intent of still supporting Python 3.8 while allowing 3.10/3.12). Re-run tox -e lint to confirm the warning is gone.
notes: Self-identified during inspection. Note: also relates to issue #165 'Fix typing' which is now OBSOLETE (verified: `tox -e static` passes 0 errors/0 warnings on HEAD with pyright 1.1.398, the version that originally triggered the 8 errors; the referenced symbols remove_prometheus_jobs/set_alert_rule_data/static_configs no longer exist in src/charm.py after refactor #240). Do not pick #165.

title: README uses deprecated Charm Store (`cs:`) deploy commands and `--series` instead of Charmhub syntax
summary: The README Deployment example tells users to run `juju deploy -m reactive cs:ubuntu --series focal -n 3` and `juju deploy -m reactive cs:telegraf`. The `cs:` prefix refers to the retired Charm Store and `--series` is the deprecated way to select a base; current Juju uses Charmhub (no prefix, or `ch:`) and `--base ubuntu@20.04`. A newcomer copy-pasting these commands will hit errors.
why_good_first_issue: Pure documentation fix in README.md, three lines, no code knowledge required; clear correct replacement (drop cs:, use --base); high value because it is the first hands-on example a new user runs.
repro_steps: On VM cos: git clone --depth 80 into ~/work; `grep -n 'cs:' README.md` and `grep -n -- '--series' README.md`; `sed -n '55,60p' README.md` to read the example block.
repro_result: Reproduced on HEAD 8bfce19. `grep -n 'cs:' README.md` -> line 29 ('cs:telegraf' in prose), line 58 ('juju deploy -m reactive cs:ubuntu --series focal -n 3'), line 59 ('juju deploy -m reactive cs:telegraf'). `grep -n -- '--series' README.md` -> line 58. Observed: deprecated Charm Store / --series syntax. Expected: Charmhub names with `--base ubuntu@20.04`.
fix_hint: Edit README.md lines 58-59 to e.g. `juju deploy -m reactive ubuntu --base ubuntu@20.04 -n 3` and `juju deploy -m reactive telegraf`; update the prose mention on line 29 from `cs:telegraf` to `telegraf`. Optionally sweep the rest of README for other `cs:` / `--series` usages while at it.
notes: Self-identified during inspection. Also noted: issue #158 ('event.app can be None on relation-broken') has been re-scoped by a maintainer comment (2025-06-20) to 'add an integration test for relation removal' - the original crash is fixed (handler _prometheus_target_relation_broken now takes `_` and no longer dereferences event.app.name, src/charm.py:258-259). #158 is therefore integration-tier (needs-live-deploy), not picked here. Issue #166 (wrongly-formatted alert rules silently not loaded) is labeled good first issue but the maintainer's proposed solutions (pydantic schema in libs / cos-tool validation / centralized status) make it broader than beginner-scope.
